No Poverty.reason: The course focuses on international trade law, which indirectly impacts economic conditions, but there is no explicit mention or direct focus on poverty eradication or related targets within the syllabus content.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Standards & health and safety: SPS & TBT' (Week 4), which directly relates to agricultural trade rules and food safety, aligning with Target 2.b on preventing trade restrictions in agricultural markets.
evidence: Standards & health and safety: SPS & TBT

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The syllabus includes the 'TRIPs Agreement' and the 'Canada—Pharmaceutical patent (DS114)' case (Week 10), directly linking to Target 3.b regarding access to affordable medicines and public health flexibilities under TRIPS.

Quality Education.reason: While the course itself is a form of tertiary education, its content does not focus on promoting quality education for all or addressing the specific targets of SDG 4.

Gender Equality.reason: There is no explicit mention or direct focus on gender equality, women's rights, or related issues within the course content or learning outcomes.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit evidence or direct connection to clean water, sanitation, water quality, or related targets is found in the syllabus.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit evidence or direct connection to affordable energy, clean energy, renewable energy, or related targets is found in the syllabus.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's core focus on 'trade liberalization' and the 'WTO framework' (Course Description) directly promotes economic growth (Target 8.1) and facilitates international business transactions, supporting productive activities and entrepreneurship (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The 'TRIPs Agreement' (Week 10) directly supports innovation (Target 9.5, 9.b). The course's focus on 'international business transaction' (Week 13) also relates to integrating enterprises into value chains and promoting industrialization (Target 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course directly addresses Target 10.a by covering the WTO's principle of special and differential treatment for developing countries, exemplified by the 'EC—tariff preferences (DS246)' case (Week 11).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit evidence or direct connection to sustainable cities, housing, transport, urbanization, or related targets is found in the syllabus.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course covers 'Trade, health, environment & general exceptions' and the 'US—shrimp (DS58)' case (Week 4), which involves trade rules related to natural resource management and environmental protection, linking to Target 12.2.

Climate Action.reason: The course's discussion of 'Trade, health, environment & general exceptions' (Week 4) provides an indirect link, as international trade law often intersects with environmental regulations, including those related to climate change (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: The 'US—shrimp (DS58)' case (Week 4) directly addresses marine conservation. Additionally, 'subsidies & countervailing duties' (Week 6) are relevant to WTO fisheries negotiations under Target 14.6.

Life on Land.reason: The 'TRIPs Agreement' (Week 10) can tangentially relate to genetic resources (Target 15.6), and 'environment & general exceptions' (Week 4) broadly touches upon terrestrial environmental issues, though not explicitly.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's central theme is the 'WTO legal system and operation' and 'dispute settlement regulations' (Course Description), which are fundamental to promoting the rule of law at international levels and building strong, accountable institutions (Targets 16.3, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is fundamentally about the 'World Trade Organization' and its role in establishing a 'rules-based, multilateral trading system' (Course Description), which directly aligns with Target 17.10.
